last_action: Referred to committee: Public Health Policy

title: Prohibit activities re: garbage-fed swine, feral swine, wild boar
description: To amend sections 901.43, 926.01, 942.01, 942.02, 943.23, 943.26, 1531.01, 1533.01, 1533.731, 1533.99, and 4745.01; to amend, for the purpose of adopting a new section number as indicated in parentheses, section 942.13 (942.05); to enact new sections 942.03 and 942.04 and sections 1533.75 and 1533.751; and to repeal sections 926.011, 942.03, 942.04, 942.05, 942.06, 942.07, 942.10, 942.11, 942.12, and 942.99 of the Revised Code to prohibit certain activities regarding garbage-fed swine, feral swine, and wild boar and to revise a definition in the Agricultural Commodity Handlers Law.

title: Enact Hayden's Law regarding alcohol server training
description: To amend section 4399.18 and to enact section 4399.19 of the Revised Code to require alcohol server training and to provide immunity from civil liability to those who successfully complete and implement the training and to name this act Hayden's Law.

title: Enact the Community Pharmacy Protection Act
description: To amend sections 3902.50, 5164.753, and 5167.243 and to enact sections 3902.75, 3902.76, 3959.151, 3959.21, 5167.127, and 5167.128 of the Revised Code regarding insurer and Medicaid program accreditation requirements for pharmacies, to impose drug cost reporting and payment requirements on pharmacy benefit managers, and to name this act the Community Pharmacy Protection Act.

title: Regards school policies on overdose reversal drugs
description: To enact sections 3313.7118, 3313.7119, 3314.148, 3326.62, and 3328.40 of the Revised Code regarding school policies on the administration of overdose reversal drugs.
last_action: Referred to committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Regards political subdivisions and specified cybersecurity
description: To amend section 1354.01 of the Revised Code to extend legal safe harbor to political subdivisions that implement a specified cybersecurity program.

title: Enact the Super Bowl Entourage Expense Act
description: To enact section 107.72 of the Revised Code to create a public records exception for certain documents related to the Governor and to name this act the Super Bowl Entourage Expense Act.

title: Apply prescription drug rebates to cost-sharing requirements
description: To enact section 3902.63 of the Revised Code to apply prescription drug rebates to cost-sharing requirements.

title: Designate Army Lt. Col. Robert R. Melnick Memorial Highway
description: To enact section 5534.648 of the Revised Code to designate a portion of State Route 11 in Mahoning County as the "Army Lt. Col. Robert R. Melnick Memorial Highway."

title: Amend the residential landlord and tenant law
description: To amend sections 5321.02, 5321.04, 5321.07, and 5321.18 of the Revised Code to amend the law regarding residential landlords and tenants.

title: Regards child emergency placement facilities, treatment solutions
description: To enact sections 121.377 and 5101.91 of the Revised Code to create a streamlined licensure process for emergency placement facilities for children and to require the Ohio Family and Children First Cabinet Council to work with stakeholders to develop treatment and placement solutions for children with developmental or intellectual challenges and high acuity behaviors.

title: Revise criminal and disciplinary provisions relating to HIV, AIDS
Require names submitted by the BMV be included on the jury list
description: To amend section 2313.06 of the Revised Code to require names submitted by the Bureau of Motor Vehicles to be included on the annual jury source list.

title: Create the Norton Music Boosters license plate
description: To amend section 4501.21 and to enact section 4503.965 of the Revised Code to create the "Norton Music Boosters" license plate.

title: Require annual transfer of funds to education licensure fund
description: To amend section 3319.51 of the Revised Code to annually require the Director of Budget and Management to transfer funds to the State Board of Education licensure fund.

title: Allow military to participate in homeownership savings program
description: To amend section 135.71 of the Revised Code to make active duty members of the uniformed services eligible to participate in the homeownership savings linked deposit program.

title: Enact the School Bus Safety Act
description: To amend sections 4503.10, 4503.102, 4503.12, 4511.75, 4511.751, and 4511.76; to amend, for the purpose of adopting a new section number as indicated in parentheses, section 4511.751 (4511.752); and to enact new section 4511.751 and sections 5.501, 4511.753, 4511.754, 4511.755, 4511.756, and 4511.757 of the Revised Code to authorize a civil penalty system related to drivers who illegally pass a school bus but cannot be identified, to designate the month of October as "School Bus Safety Awareness Month," and to designate this act as the School Bus Safety Act.
last_action: Reported - Re-referred to Civil Justice: Rules and Reference

title: Restore the Clean Ohio Fund and provide for its administration
description: To amend section 151.40 and to enact sections 122.65, 122.651, 122.652, 122.653, 122.654, 122.655, 122.656, 122.657, 122.658, and 122.659 of the Revised Code to restore the Clean Ohio Fund to be administered by the Department of Development and the Clean Ohio Council.

title: Allow sports or athletic activity to fulfill phys ed requirement
description: To amend section 3313.603 of the Revised Code to permit students to use club sports or other athletic activity to fulfill the high school physical education requirement.
last_action: Referred to committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Address minors working as performers in entertainment industry
description: To amend sections 3331.01, 3331.12, 4109.01, 4109.03, 4109.05, 4109.06, and 4109.08 and to enact sections 4109.25, 4109.26, 4109.27, 4109.28, and 4109.29 of the Revised Code to address minors working as performers in the entertainment industry.
last_action: Referred to committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Enact the Repeat Offender Act

title: Regards info poster on benefits and services for veterans
description: To enact section 4113.13 of the Revised Code regarding the creation and display of a poster containing information on benefits and services for veterans.
last_action: Referred to committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Enact the Ohio Fairness Act
description: To amend sections 4112.01 and 4112.05 of the Revised Code to enact the Ohio Fairness Act to prohibit discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity or expression, to add mediation as an informal method that the Ohio Civil Rights Commission may use, and to uphold existing religious exemptions under Ohio's Civil Rights Law.

title: Regards assault when the victim is a school employee or volunteer
description: To amend section 2903.13 of the Revised Code regarding the penalty for assault when the victim is a school employee or volunteer.

title: Regards pass-through of Ohio Works First support payments
description: To amend sections 5107.05, 5107.10, and 5107.20 of the Revised Code regarding the pass-through of support payments under the Ohio Works First program.

title: Create NASA Glenn Research Center license plate
description: To enact section 4503.587 of the Revised Code to create the "NASA Glenn Research Center" license plate.

title: Increase criminal penalty for intimidating an election officer
description: To amend section 3599.24 of the Revised Code to increase the criminal penalty for intimidating an election officer.

title: Enact Atmosphere Protection Act
description: To amend section 3704.99 and to enact sections 3704.21, 3704.22, and 3704.23 of the Revised Code to prohibit a person from engaging in solar radiation modification and to name the act the Atmosphere Protection Act.
last_action: Referred to committee: Energy and Natural Resources

title: Provide smoking ban exemption for cigar bars
description: To amend sections 3794.01 and 3794.03 of the Revised Code to provide an exemption from the smoking ban for cigar bars.

title: Enact Braden's Law to prohibit sexual extortion
description: To amend sections 2137.15, 2905.11, 2929.12, and 2933.51 and to enact sections 2905.111 and 2933.523 of the Revised Code to enact Braden's Law to prohibit sexual extortion and to require service providers to comply with search warrants and interception warrants for electronic information.

description: To amend sections 2903.13 and 2935.01 of the Revised Code to expand the definition of "peace officer" to include bailiffs of a municipal court and probation officers, and to increase the penalty for assault when the victim is a bailiff, probation officer, or another court official or employee.

title: Create the Iota Phi Theta license plate
description: To amend section 4501.21 and to enact section 4503.512 of the Revised Code to create the "Iota Phi Theta" license plate.

title: Enact the Lead Line Replacement Act
description: To amend sections 6109.01 and 6109.22 and to enact sections 504.201, 735.274, 735.291, 743.041, 6101.531, 6103.021, 6109.09, 6109.091, 6109.092, 6109.093, 6109.094, 6109.095, 6109.096, 6109.097, 6109.098, 6109.099, 6109.0910, 6115.531, and 6119.062 of the Revised Code to establish a program regarding lead service line replacement and to name this act the Lead Line Replacement Act.
last_action: Referred to committee: Energy and Natural Resources

title: Create an interim driver's license for pending visa applicants
description: To enact section 4507.064 of the Revised Code to create an interim limited term driver's license for pending visa applicants.

title: Prohibit advertising a price without all mandatory charges
description: To enact section 1345.023 of the Revised Code to prohibit a supplier from advertising, displaying, or offering a price for a good or service that does not include all mandatory fees and charges.
last_action: Referred to committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Designate Lupus Awareness Month
description: To enact section 5.56 of the Revised Code to designate May as "Lupus Awareness Month."

title: Revise the law governing high volume dog breeders
description: To amend sections 956.01, 956.031, and 956.10 of the Revised Code to revise the law governing high volume dog breeders.

title: Enact the Consumer Protection Call Center Act
description: To enact sections 4113.87, 4113.88, 4113.89, 4113.90, 4113.91, and 4113.92 of the Revised Code to enact the Consumer Protection Call Center Act regarding the eligibility of employers that relocate a call center to a foreign country to receive state grants, loans, and other benefits.
last_action: Referred to committee: Commerce and Labor

description: To amend sections 4109.08, 4109.13, and 4109.99 of the Revised Code to require school employees to report certain minor labor law violations, to increase penalties for certain minor labor law violations, to create the Minor Labor Law Enforcement Fund, and to make an appropriation.
last_action: Referred to committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Create the Blanchester Wildcats license plate
description: To amend section 4501.21 and to enact section 4503.894 of the Revised Code to create the "Blanchester Wildcats" license plate.

title: Regards property tax payments, sale of tax-foreclosed property

last_action: Referred to committee: Ways and Means

description: To amend sections 2911.211, 2917.12, 2923.04, 2927.12, 4511.16, 4511.17, and 5589.99 and to enact sections 2911.212 and 5589.34 of the Revised Code to prohibit unauthorized displays of symbols on bridges or highways and unauthorized projections of images onto buildings and to expand the offenses of aggravated trespass and ethnic intimidation.

title: Regulate the practice of midwives
last_action: Referred to committee: Health Provider Services

title: Require Ohio election ballots to have an identifying watermark
description: To amend sections 3505.28, 3506.01, and 3509.07 and to enact section 3506.022 of the Revised Code to require all ballots in Ohio elections to have an identifying watermark.

description: To enact section 175.061 of the Revised Code to limit state low-income housing tax credits to projects that verify tenant immigration status.
last_action: Reported - Amended: Economic and Workforce Development

title: Prohibit certain sales types in real property foreclosure sales

title: Authorize county to implement motor fuel quality testing program
description: To amend section 1327.70 of the Revised Code to authorize a county to implement a motor fuel quality testing program.

title: Require health insurers to cover certain preventive screenings
description: To enact section 3902.64 of the Revised Code to require health insurers to cover preventive screenings for certain men at high-risk for developing prostate cancer.

title: Prohibit firearm possession by persons unlawfully present in U.S.
description: To amend sections 2923.13 and 2923.14 of the Revised Code to prohibit persons who are unlawfully present in the United States from possessing firearms.

title: Enact the Voter Verification Act
description: To amend sections 3501.01, 3503.151, 3503.19, 3503.21, 3505.181, 3505.182, and 3505.183 and to enact section 3503.201 of the Revised Code to enact the Voter Verification Act to require election officials to verify certain information in voter registration records and to require electors to provide corrected information.

title: Regulate the provision of earned wage access services
description: To amend section 1321.21 and to enact sections 1320.01, 1320.02, 1320.03, 1320.04, 1320.05, 1320.06, 1320.07, 1320.08, 1320.09, and 1320.10 of the Revised Code to regulate the provision of earned wage access services.
last_action: Referred to committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Establish a process for sale of abandoned or derelict aircraft
description: To amend sections 1311.71, 1311.72, 1311.73, 1311.75, 1311.76, 1311.77, and 4561.01 and to enact sections 1311.721, 4561.26, and 4561.27 of the Revised Code to establish a process by which an abandoned or derelict aircraft may be sold.

title: Prohibit certain public nuisance actions
description: To amend sections 715.44 and 2307.71 and to enact section 2307.801 of the Revised Code to prohibit certain public nuisance actions.

title: Create criminal liability for teachers for pandering obscenity
description: To amend sections 2907.32 and 2907.35 of the Revised Code to create criminal liability for certain teachers and librarians for the offense of pandering obscenity.

title: Regards school directory information and public records
description: To amend sections 149.381, 149.43, and 3319.321 of the Revised Code to permit schools to withhold directory information and to remove directory information from the public record definition.

title: Require food service operations to denote certain allergens
description: To enact section 3717.54 of the Revised Code to require food service operations to denote certain allergens on their menu.
last_action: Referred to committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Regards workers' comp-emergency workers' psychiatric conditions
description: To amend sections 2929.14, 2941.1414, 4123.01, 4123.026, and 4123.46; to enact section 4123.87; and to repeal section 126.65 of the Revised Code concerning workers' compensation coverage for peace officers, firefighters, and emergency medical workers diagnosed with psychiatric conditions arising from employment without an accompanying physical injury.

description: To amend sections 3314.01, 3314.02, 3314.032, and 3314.05 and to enact section 3314.0111 of the Revised Code to prohibit for-profit operators of community schools.
last_action: Referred to committee: Primary and Secondary Education

description: To enact section 1509.101 of the Revised Code to require horizontal oil and gas well owners that drill in state parks to disclose the chemical components of the materials used in the drilling operation.
last_action: Referred to committee: Energy and Natural Resources

title: Enact the Future Educators Support Act

description: To amend section 4510.037 of the Revised Code to shorten the time that points remain on a person's commercial driver's license or permit and to allow such drivers to take the remedial driving course more frequently.
last_action: Referred to committee: Commerce and Labor

title: Enact the EdChoice Fair Fiscal Responsibility Act
description: To amend sections 3310.03, 3310.032, 3310.13, 3310.16, 3310.41, 3310.52, 3313.975, 3313.976, 3313.978, 3317.022, and 3365.07, to repeal sections 3310.035 and 3310.08 of the Revised Code, and to repeal Sections 265.275, 265.277, and 265.571 of H.B. 33 of the 135th General Assembly to enact the EdChoice Fair Fiscal Responsibility Act to revise the operations of the Educational Choice Scholarship Pilot Program.
last_action: Referred to committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Create the Healthy Soils Task Force
description: To create the Healthy Soils Task Force.

title: Reinstate the Joint Education Oversight Committee
description: To enact sections 103.44, 103.45, 103.46, 103.47, 103.48, 103.49, and 103.50 of the Revised Code and to repeal Section 733.40 of H.B. 166 of the 133rd General Assembly to reinstate the Joint Education Oversight Committee and to make an appropriation.

description: To amend sections 3314.03, 3326.11, 3328.24, and 3345.37 and to enact sections 3313.473, 3345.371, and 3345.87 of the Revised Code to require schools and higher education institutions to include the national suicide and crisis lifeline telephone number on student identification cards, student planners, and electronic portals, and to require higher education institutions to provide information about declarations for mental health treatment.

description: To enact section 3333.0419 of the Revised Code to require the Chancellor of Higher Education to conduct a survey of teacher and administrator preparation programs to determine what instruction they are providing to students on mental and behavioral health, behavior management, and classroom management, and to make recommendations.

description: To amend section 131.51 of the Revised Code to increase the proportion of state tax revenue allocated to the Local Government Fund from 1.7% to 3.68% beginning July 1, 2026.

title: Allow board of ed employees to use sick leave for parental leave
description: To amend sections 124.38 and 3319.141 of the Revised Code to allow board of education employees to use accrued sick leave for a parental leave of absence.
last_action: Referred to committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Enact the Private School Accountability and Transparency Act
description: To amend sections 3301.0711, 3301.16, 3301.164, 3310.03, 3310.14, 3310.15, 3310.522, 3313.619, and 3313.976 and to enact sections 3301.141, 3301.165, 3301.166, 3301.167, 3301.168, 3310.101, and 3310.151 of the Revised Code to enact the Private School Accountability and Transparency Act regarding financial, reporting, and other requirements applicable to chartered nonpublic schools.
last_action: Referred to committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Authorize tax credits for certain employer-provided child care

last_action: Referred to committee: Ways and Means

title: Authorize income tax credit for certain child care expenses
description: To amend sections 5747.08 and 5747.98 and to enact section 5747.74 of the Revised Code to authorize a refundable income tax credit for certain child care expenses.
last_action: Referred to committee: Ways and Means

title: Authorize income tax credit-contributions to child care programs
description: To amend section 5747.98 and to enact section 5747.86 of the Revised Code to authorize a nonrefundable income tax credit for contributions to certain child care programs.
last_action: Referred to committee: Ways and Means

title: Allow township to levy a tax to pay for school resource officers
description: To enact section 5705.231 of the Revised Code to allow a township to levy a tax to pay for school district resource officers.
last_action: Referred to committee: Ways and Means

title: Allow publicly funded child care for foster, kinship caregivers
description: To amend section 5104.30 and to enact section 5104.302 of the Revised Code to make foster caregivers and kinship caregivers eligible for publicly funded child care and to amend the version of section 5104.30 of the Revised Code that is scheduled to take effect on January 1, 2025, to continue the changes on and after that date.

title: Enact the Charter School Oversight Act
description: To enact sections 3314.272, 3314.30, 3314.31, and 3326.171 of the Revised Code to enact the Charter School Oversight Act.
last_action: Referred to committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Enact the School Busing Improvement Act
description: To amend sections 3317.0212, 3317.071, 3327.01, and 3327.016; to enact new section 3327.021; and to repeal section 3327.021 of the Revised Code to enact the School Busing Improvement Act regarding transportation of students and to make an appropriation.
last_action: Referred to committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Express intent to increase accountability for group homes
Create Minority Law Student Encouragement Scholarship Pilot Prgm
description: To establish the Minority Law Student Encouragement Scholarship Pilot Program and to make an appropriation.

title: Promote food options-students with religious diet restrictions
description: To make an appropriation to promote food options for students with religious dietary restrictions.

title: Create Weirdo Cat Lovers of Cleveland license plate
description: To amend section 4501.21 and to enact section 4503.943 of the Revised Code to create the "Weirdo Cat Lovers of Cleveland" license plate.

title: Regards certain forms of solicitation-Medicare supplement policy
description: To amend section 3923.332 of the Revised Code to prohibit the Superintendent of Insurance from prohibiting certain forms of solicitation of Medicare supplement policies.

title: Regards drug reimbursements made to 340B covered entities
description: To enact section 4729.521 of the Revised Code to prohibit drug manufacturers and wholesalers from taking certain actions regarding reimbursements made to 340B covered entities.
last_action: Referred to committee: Public Health Policy

title: Prohibit executing death sentence by nitrogen gas administration
description: To amend section 2949.22 of the Revised Code to prohibit the state from executing a death sentence by administration of nitrogen gas.

title: Enact the Hunger-Free Campus Act
description: To enact section 3333.47 of the Revised Code to enact the Hunger-Free Campus Act to establish the Hunger-Free Campus Grant Program and designation and to make an appropriation.

title: Enact the Charter School Sponsor and Operator Rating Act
description: To amend sections 3314.012, 3314.016, 3314.021, and 3314.031 and to enact section 3314.0111 of the Revised Code to enact the Charter School Sponsor and Operator Rating Act regarding report cards for community school sponsors and operators.
last_action: Referred to committee: Primary and Secondary Education

title: Enact the School Rating Enhancement Act
description: To enact sections 3301.165, 3302.039, and 3302.0311 of the Revised Code to enact the School Rating Enhancement Act to establish state report cards for chartered nonpublic schools that enroll state scholarship students and to establish the State Report Card Review Committee.
last_action: Referred to committee: Primary and Secondary Education

description: To amend sections 2905.05 and 2950.01 of the Revised Code to require that a person act with a sexual motivation or an unlawful purpose to commit the offense of criminal child enticement.

last_action: Referred to committee: Economic and Workforce Development

title: Require coverage of epinephrine and glucagon for minors

last_action: Referred to committee: Ways and Means

title: Designate the walleye as the official fish
description: To enact section 5.037 of the Revised Code to designate the walleye as the official fish of the state.
last_action: Referred to committee: Agriculture and Natural Resources

description: To amend sections 733.72 and 733.77 of the Revised Code to require a party filing a complaint against a municipal officer to pay the officer's legal expenses if the officer is acquitted.

last_action: Referred to committee: Public Health Policy

title: Urge Congress enact law re: trains carrying hazardous materials
description: To urge the United States Congress to pass legislation requiring railroad companies and the U.S. Department of Transportation to inform local and state government officials when trains carrying potentially hazardous materials travel through their respective jurisdictions.
last_action: Introduced and Referred to Committee: Government Oversight

description: To support and encourage the work of the Ohio Commission for the United States Semiquincentennial in celebrating the nation's 250th anniversary.

title: Urge Congress to repeal Windfall Elimination
description: To urge Congress to repeal the Windfall Elimination Provision and the Government Pension Offset.

title: Urge Congress to make daylight saving time permanent
description: To urge Congress to make daylight saving time the permanent standard time.

title: Urge selection of Ohio as headquarters of the U.S. Space Command
description: To urge the federal government to select Ohio for the permanent headquarters of the United States Space Command.

title: Adopt Restoration of America's Readiness (ROAR) Resolution
description: To urge the United States Congress to restore the Department of Defense's superior warfighting principles of recruiting, assigning, training, promoting, and retaining personnel solely based on merit and ensuring such personnel maintain and display a warrior ethos, and to name this resolution the "Restoration of America's Readiness (ROAR) Resolution."
last_action: Introduced and Referred to Committee: Homeland Security

title: Recognize lifeguard shortage impact; encourage lifeguard careers
description: To recognize the impact of the lifeguard shortage in Ohio and encourage individuals to consider a career as a lifeguard.

title: Condemn People's Republic of China for global drug trade role
description: To condemn the People's Republic of China for its role in the global drug trade, especially in its capacity as the largest point of origin of fentanyl smuggled to the United States.

title: Express that Ohio stands with Texas; denounce open-border policy
description: To express that Ohio stands with Texas and to denounce President Biden's administration's open-border policies.
last_action: Introduced and Referred to Committee: Government Oversight

title: Urge inclusion of Peter "Pete" Rose on MLB Hall of Fame ballot
description: To urge the Commissioner of Major League Baseball to remove Peter Edward "Pete" Rose from Major League Baseball's permanently ineligible list as soon as possible, and to urge the Baseball Writers' Association of America and the National Baseball Hall of Fame to include Rose on the Hall of Fame ballot.
last_action: Introduced and Referred to Committee: Government Oversight

description: To amend sections 2923.13 and 2923.14 of the Revised Code to prohibit a person who is charged with or has been convicted of first degree misdemeanor domestic violence from possessing a firearm or dangerous ordnance.

title: Limit liability in regards to firearm hold agreements
description: To enact section 2923.22 of the Revised Code to limit liability for federally licensed firearms dealers in regards to firearm hold agreements.

title: Authorize tax credit for handgun training and firearms storage
description: To amend sections 5747.08 and 5747.98 and to enact sections 5747.86 and 5747.87 of the Revised Code to authorize a nonrefundable income tax credit for the cost of handgun training and firearms storage and locking devices.
last_action: Referred to committee: Ways and Means

description: To amend section 5753.021 of the Revised Code to reduce the sports gaming tax rate from 20% to 10%.

title: Designate PFC Richard 'Rick' Wesley McFarland Memorial Highway
description: To enact section 5534.154 of the Revised Code to designate a portion of State Route 39 in Richland County as the "PFC Richard 'Rick' Wesley McFarland Memorial Highway."

description: To amend section 742.33 of the Revised Code to increase contribution amounts that employers of full-time municipal police officers must make to the Ohio Police and Fire Pension Fund.

title: Establish Ohio Ireland Trade Commission
description: To enact sections 122.29, 122.291, 122.292, 122.293, and 149.60 of the Revised Code to establish the Ohio Ireland Trade Commission and the Ohio River Commission of Ohio.

title: Regards authority of advanced practice registered nurses
Allow for the sealing or expungement of certain charges

title: Provide inmates with state ID cards, education documentation
description: To amend sections 4507.50, 4507.51, 4507.52, 5120.59, and 5139.511 and to enact section 5145.1611 of the Revised Code and to amend Section 383.10 of H.B. 33 of the 135th General Assembly to provide inmates with state identification cards and documentation upon their release relating to work experience, education, and trade skills, and make retroactive changes to the local jail facility funding program.

title: Revise the Securities Law
description: To amend sections 1707.01, 1707.03, 1707.09, 1707.091, 1707.092, 1707.13, and 1707.23 of the Revised Code to make changes to the Securities Law.
last_action: Referred to committee: Financial Institutions and Technology

description: To declare the General Assembly's intent to regulate carbon capture and storage technologies and the geologic sequestration of carbon dioxide for long-term storage.

title: Put Supreme Court, appeals court candidate on nonpartisan ballot
description: To amend sections 3501.01, 3505.03, 3505.04, and 3513.257 of the Revised Code to require justice of the Supreme Court and judge of court of appeals candidates to appear on the nonpartisan ballot with other judicial candidates.

title: Regards courts and court procedures
Regulate earned wage access services
description: To amend section 1321.21 and to enact sections 1320.01, 1320.02, 1320.03, 1320.04, 1320.05, 1320.06, 1320.07, 1320.08, 1320.09, and 1320.10 of the Revised Code to regulate the provision of earned wage access services.

title: Establish the Nonchartered Educational Savings Account Program
description: To amend sections 3317.02, 3317.022, 3317.03, and 5747.75 and to enact sections 3310.21, 3310.22, 3310.23, 3310.24, 3310.25, and 3310.26 of the Revised Code to establish the Nonchartered Educational Savings Account Program.

title: Change Lebanon Municipal Court judge from part-time to full-time
description: To amend section 1901.08 of the Revised Code to change the status of the judge of the Lebanon Municipal Court from part-time to full-time.

title: Modify the Motor Vehicle Sales Law re: recreational vehicles
Regards items that accelerate a firearm's rate of fire
description: To amend sections 2923.13 and 2923.14 and to enact section 2923.133 of the Revised Code to prohibit certain conduct regarding trigger cranks, bump-fire devices, and other items that accelerate a semi-automatic firearm's rate of fire but do not convert it into an automatic firearm.
last_action: Referred to committee: Veterans and Public Safety

title: Regards school district property taxes and millage calculations
description: To amend sections 319.301 and 5705.314 of the Revised Code to limit the ability of school districts to reallocate unvoted property tax millage and to include emergency levies and substitute levies in the calculation of a school district's effective millage floor.
last_action: Referred to committee: Ways and Means

title: Allow community foundations as trustee of school charitable funds
description: To amend sections 1715.51, 3315.10, and 3315.41 and to enact sections 3314.55, 3315.43, 3326.53, and 3328.53 of the Revised Code to permit community foundations to be trustees of endowments and other charitable funds received by public schools and to classify some funds held by community foundation trustees as institutional funds.

title: Designate U.S.S. Shenandoah Memorial Highway
description: To enact section 5534.672 of the Revised Code to designate a portion of I-77 in Noble County as the "U.S.S. Shenandoah Memorial Highway."

title: Permit students to concurrently receive certain scholarships
description: To amend sections 3310.034 and 3310.51 of the Revised Code to permit students to concurrently receive an Educational Choice scholarship or Pilot Project scholarship and either an Autism or Jon Peterson Special Needs scholarship.

title: Legalize and regulate internet casino gaming; levy a tax
Enact Alyssa's Law regarding school wearable panic alert systems
description: To amend sections 3314.03, 3326.11, and 3328.24 and to enact section 3313.88 of the Revised Code to enact Alyssa's Law to require public and chartered nonpublic schools to implement a wearable panic alert system and to make an appropriation.

title: Regards electronic monitoring in a long-term care facility
description: To amend sections 3721.60, 3721.61, 3721.62, 3721.63, 3721.65, and 3721.66 of the Revised Code to make changes regarding electronic monitoring of a resident's room in a long-term care facility.

title: Designate CPL Willard Boston Wallace Memorial Highway
description: To enact section 5534.674 of the Revised Code to designate a portion of State Route 781 in Adams County as the "CPL Willard Boston Wallace Memorial Highway."

title: Provide sales tax rebates to music venues, festival promoters
description: To amend section 5739.21 and to enact section 122.97 of the Revised Code to create a music incubator program to provide sales tax rebates to certain music venues and festival promoters.
last_action: Referred to committee: Ways and Means

title: Permit fee payment by cryptocurrency; cryptocurrency investments
description: To amend sections 9.16, 145.11, 742.11, 3307.15, 3309.15, and 5505.06 and to enact sections 3345.161 and 5703.83 of the Revised Code to permit payment of state and local taxes and other government fees with cryptocurrency and to address investments in cryptocurrency by state colleges and universities and the state retirement systems.
last_action: Referred to committee: Financial Institutions and Technology

title: Regards alcohol, drug addiction, mental health service districts
description: To amend section 340.07 of the Revised Code to authorize county commissioners to act in the place of a board of certain alcohol, drug addiction, and mental health service districts when managing and spending the budget of the district.

title: Require proof of citizenship to register to vote
description: To amend sections 3501.01, 3503.01, 3503.06, 3503.13, 3503.14, 3503.15, 3503.151, 3503.152, 3503.153, 3503.16, 3503.19, 3503.21, 3505.18, 3505.181, 3505.182, 3505.183, 3505.20, 3509.04, 3509.05, 3509.06, 3509.07, 3509.08, 3511.04, 3511.05, 3511.09, 3511.14, and 3511.16 and to enact section 3503.201 of the Revised Code to require proof of citizenship to register to vote or to update a voter registration and to designate an elector who registers on a federal form without providing proof of citizenship as a federal-only voter.

title: Require an agency store to open and reseal spirituous liquor
description: To amend section 4301.62 and to enact section 4301.173 of the Revised Code to require an agency store to open and reseal an allocated bottle of spirituous liquor prior to the bottle's retail sale.
last_action: Referred to committee: Agriculture and Natural Resources

title: Regards liability for injury caused by consumption of food
description: To enact section 2307.56 of the Revised Code to provide for a reasonable expectation test in determining liability for injury caused by consumption of food containing a substance injurious to health and for the jury to make that determination.

title: Regards freestanding birth centers
description: To enact section 3702.35 of the Revised Code regarding freestanding birth centers.

title: Designate September 28 as Speaker Jo Ann Davidson Day
description: To enact section 5.56 of the Revised Code to designate September 28 as "Speaker Jo Ann Davidson Day."
last_action: Referred to committee: State and Local Government

title: Require citizenship proof to register to vote;revise election law
description: To amend sections 303.12, 303.59, 307.94, 3501.01, 3501.38, 3501.381, 3501.39, 3503.01, 3503.13, 3503.14, 3503.15, 3503.151, 3503.152, 3503.16, 3503.19, 3503.21, 3505.181, 3505.182, 3505.183, 3505.20, 3509.05, 3509.08, 3513.07, 3513.261, 3517.01, 3517.12, 3519.02, 3519.05, 3519.16, and 3599.21 and to enact sections 3503.201 and 3503.202 of the Revised Code to require proof of citizenship to register to vote and to modify procedures regarding voter roll maintenance, absent voting, and election petitions.

title: Regards allocation of parenting responsibilities
Prohibit sale of intoxicating hemp products
description: To amend sections 928.01, 928.03, 3719.01, 5502.01, 5502.13, and 5502.14 and to enact sections 3779.01, 3779.02, 3779.03, 3779.04, 3779.05, and 3779.99 of the Revised Code to prohibit the sale of intoxicating hemp products and to declare an emergency.

description: To enact sections 1357.01, 1357.02, 1357.04, 1357.05, 1357.06, 1357.07, 1357.08, 1357.09, 1357.10, 1357.11, 1357.12, and 1357.13 of the Revised Code to create a regulatory relief office within the Department of Commerce and establish a universal regulatory sandbox program under which the office may temporarily waive certain state laws and regulations and allow businesses to demonstrate innovative products and services.

title: Regulate use of pricing algorithms
description: To amend sections 1331.01, 1331.04, and 1331.16 and to enact sections 1331.05 and 1331.50 of the Revised Code to regulate the use of pricing algorithms.
committee: Senate Small Business and Economic Opportunity
last_action: Referred to committee: Small Business and Economic Opportunity

title: Enact the School Bus Safety Act
description: To amend sections 4503.10, 4503.102, 4503.12, 4511.75, 4511.751, and 4511.76; to amend, for the purpose of adopting a new section number as indicated in parentheses, section 4511.751 (4511.752); and to enact new section 4511.751 and sections 5.501, 3327.18, 3327.19, 4511.753, 4511.754, 4511.755, 4511.756, and 4511.757 of the Revised Code to authorize a civil penalty system related to drivers who illegally pass a school bus but cannot be identified, to designate the month of August as "School Bus Safety Awareness Month," and to designate this act as the School Bus Safety Act.
last_action: Referred to committee: Veterans and Public Safety

title: Revise the telemarketing law
description: To amend section 109.87 of the Revised Code to make changes to the telemarketing law.

title: Regards recording liens and other real property instruments

title: Create Tri-Share Child Care Pilot Program
description: To create the Tri-Share Child Care Pilot Program.
